headlines.title: Spanish government confirms new case of hantavirus
headlines.description: The Spanish government on Monday said it has confirmed a new case of hantavirus connected to the cruise ship MV Hondius, which became the center of an outbreak that killed three people earlier this month

headlines.title: Ebola patients flee in attacks on DR Congo health facilities, hobbling response
headlines.description: Doctors operating on the front lines of the fight against Ebola in Congo are having to deal with attacks on their facilities and fleeing patients.

headlines.title: Absence of USAID likely slowed Ebola detection and response, former officials say
headlines.description: The Trump administration hollowed out USAID last year, but the State Department denied the changes have hampered its response.

headlines.title: From Omaha Steaks to hazmat suits: What life was like for contagion patients in quarantine
headlines.description: As more than 40 Americans remain in quarantine for up to six weeks following a hantavirus outbreak aboard a cruise ship, former patients who spent time inside some of the country’s highest-security medical isolation units during previous viral contagions are sharing what it’s like to endure weeks cut off from the outside world.

headlines.title: Good news: You don’t need surgery. But you’ll have to apply maggots to your wound.
headlines.description: The lowly maggot gets a bad rap, mostly known for feeding on corpses and rotting meat.

headlines.title: Tips to Protect Yourself From Ticks and Mosquitos This Summer
headlines.description: Mosquitos and ticks are back for the summer so NBC News medical contributor Dr. John Torres joins TODAY’s Laura Jarrett to break down how to best protect yourself from bugs this summer. He shares tips for preparing your home by trimming tall grass and installing screens on windows, breaks down what to do if you find a tick on your clothing or body, and warns of the different types of diseases mosquitos and ticks carry.

headlines.title: Crack Found on Chemical Tank Could Lower Explosion Risk
headlines.description: A massive tank filled with a dangerous toxic chemical is at risk of exploding and has forced nearly 50,000 people from their Orange County, California, homes. Authorities are now trying to verify if a crack they found could potentially alleviate the pressure in the tank. NBC’s Steve Patterson reports for TODAY.

headlines.title: These Twin Brothers Work as Doctors at the Same Hospital
headlines.description: Dr. Andre Cheng and his identical twin Sandre both work at New York’s Long Island Jewish Hospital in Forest Hills fulfilling their dream – and their mother’s dream – as doctors. Their bond comes with a built-in second opinion and a connection that helps in stressful times. NBC’s Hallie Jackson reports in this week’s Sunday Spotlight.

headlines.title: Toxic Chemical Tank Threat Forces Evacuations in California
headlines.description: A huge industrial tank filled with a toxic chemical is at risk of exploding in Orange County, California, and efforts to cool the tank and buy more time are failing. The situation has put 40,000 thousand people under mandatory evacuation orders and Gov. Gavin Newsom declared a state of emergency. NBC’s Steve Patterson reports for TODAY.

headlines.title: Is the Forme Power Bra really worth $179?
headlines.description: I tested the Forme Power Bra for five months to see if it could improve my posture and back support. Here’s what actually happened.

headlines.title: The best running watches, tested over hundreds of miles
headlines.description: I’ve tested dozens of running watches from Garmin, Coros, Amazfit, Fitbit, Apple and more. Here are my top picks for beginners, races and ultra-athletes alike.

headlines.title: The best men’s walking shoes of 2026, tested and reviewed
headlines.description: Shop expert-recommended and staff-tested men’s walking shoes from Brooks, On, New Balance and Hoka.
